channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: ChatGPT-5: Top 10 Issues  #artificialintelligence #chatgpt #shorts #ai
executive_summary: The video discusses the top 10 issues with ChatGPT-5, highlighting its reliance on a hidden routing system that often defaults to less capable models. Users can improve performance by using explicit prompts and customizing instructions. Key challenges include model drift, long context management, and JSON reliability, which require strategic prompting and careful monitoring.
bullets: ChatGPT-5 uses a hidden routing system that often defaults to a faster but less capable model, which can lead to shallow responses. This is like a restaurant that serves fast food instead of gourmet meals when it's busy. Users can counter this by adding explicit prompts like 'think hard' to encourage deeper analysis.

API users can directly select models, while chat users rely on the routing system, which can lead to inconsistencies. This is akin to a train that only stops at certain stations for passengers, while others can choose their stops. Pro plans offer limited model selection, so understanding how to steer prompts is crucial for chat users.

Upgrades to GPT-5 can disrupt established workflows, a phenomenon known as model drift. Imagine a road that suddenly changes direction; if you don’t have a map, you could easily get lost. Keeping a version history of prompts and re-testing with new models is essential for maintaining stability.

Even with larger token windows, perfect recall is not guaranteed in ChatGPT-5. This is like trying to remember every detail of a long movie without taking notes; you might miss key plot points. Techniques like U-shaped prompting and repeated context reminders are vital for effective long-context management.

GPT-5’s smaller variants may return broken JSON or falsely claim tool actions, which can lead to errors in data processing. It's like a delivery service that sometimes sends the wrong package; you need to double-check what you receive. To mitigate this, users should make explicit JSON schema requests and require proof artifacts.

Reasoning mode in ChatGPT-5 takes longer and uses more tokens, which can increase costs. Think of it like hiring a consultant who charges by the hour; the more complex the questions, the longer it takes. Users can adjust personality settings to maintain warmth or empathy without always resorting to deep reasoning.

Scientific queries that approach safety boundaries may trigger conservative completions in ChatGPT-5. This is similar to a safety net that restricts how far you can go on a tightrope; it limits your options. Users on lower-tier plans may experience silent downgrades after heavy use, so monitoring usage is crucial to avoid quality drops.

The quote 'We have to teach the stochastic people spirit what we need from it' emphasizes the importance of clear communication with AI models. It’s like teaching a dog commands; if you’re vague, it won’t understand what you want. Clear prompts lead to better outcomes.

Prompting is described as a durable skill, meaning it requires practice and adaptation as models change. This is like learning to ride a bike; you need to adjust your balance as you gain speed. Evolving workflows is essential to keep up with advancements in AI technology.

The expectation that AI will understand vague English is unrealistic, as stated in the quote, 'The idea that the magic rocks that think will understand exactly what you want in your vague English is not reality.' This is like expecting a foreign language speaker to understand slang without context; clarity is key.

Maintaining a history of prompts is crucial for stability in workflows, especially when models are updated. This is like keeping a diary to track your progress; without it, you might forget important lessons learned. Regularly revisiting and updating prompts helps adapt to changes in AI behavior.

Explicitly steering prompts is necessary for effective use of ChatGPT-5, especially for chat users. Think of it like guiding a ship; without a clear direction, it can drift off course. Users must learn to craft their prompts carefully to achieve desired results.
key_takeaways: Use explicit prompts like 'think hard' to counter shallow responses from ChatGPT-5. This matters because it encourages deeper analysis and improves the quality of interactions. If you skip this, you risk receiving surface-level answers that don't meet your needs.

Maintain a version history of your prompts to manage model drift effectively. This is crucial for ensuring that your workflows remain stable and reliable as models are updated. Without this practice, you may find your established processes disrupted and inefficient.

Regularly test your prompts with new models to adapt to changes in AI behavior. This should be done every time there's an upgrade to ensure that your interactions remain effective. Failing to do so could lead to unexpected results and frustration in your work.

Monitor your usage on lower-tier plans to avoid silent downgrades in model quality. This is important because it helps maintain the performance you expect from ChatGPT-5. If ignored, you may experience a decline in response quality without realizing it.

technical_terms.ChatGPT-5: The latest version of OpenAI's conversational AI model, which uses a multi-model routing system to generate responses. Think of it like a multi-lane highway where traffic can switch lanes based on speed and capacity.
technical_terms.Model Drift: A phenomenon where updates to AI models can disrupt previously established workflows. Like a river changing course, it can lead to unexpected challenges if you're not prepared.
technical_terms.JSON: A lightweight data interchange format that is easy for humans to read and write, and easy for machines to parse and generate. Think of it like a standardized recipe that everyone can follow to create the same dish.
technical_terms.Prompt Engineering: The practice of crafting specific prompts to guide AI responses effectively. Like a sculptor chiseling away at stone, the right prompts shape the output into something useful.
technical_terms.Reasoning Mode: A mode in ChatGPT-5 that allows for deeper analysis but consumes more tokens and time. It's like a deep dive into a pool; it takes longer but can yield richer insights.
technical_terms.Guardrails: Safety measures in AI that limit the types of responses generated, especially in sensitive areas. Think of them like guardrails on a mountain road, preventing vehicles from going off the edge.
technical_terms.Token: A unit of text that the AI processes, which can be a word or part of a word. Like a building block, each token contributes to the overall structure of the response.
technical_terms.U-shaped Prompting: A technique that involves structuring prompts to enhance context retention. It's like building a bridge with supports at both ends to ensure stability.
technical_terms.Personality Settings: Customizable options in ChatGPT-5 that allow users to adjust the tone and style of responses. Think of it like choosing the mood lighting in a room; it changes the atmosphere significantly.
technical_terms.API: An application programming interface that allows users to interact directly with the AI model. Like a remote control that lets you operate a device from a distance, it provides more control over functionality.
technical_terms.Routing System: The mechanism that determines which model to use for generating responses in ChatGPT-5. It's like a traffic director at an intersection, deciding which cars go where based on conditions.
technical_terms.Proof Artifacts: Evidence or outputs generated by AI that confirm the accuracy of tool actions. Think of them like receipts that verify a purchase; they provide proof of what was done.
